reasoning: The 10-2 yield spread has trended strongly positive over the last month (0.35 to 0.67) before dipping to 0.55 recently. The linear trend model suggests a high likelihood (P=0.75) of the value rebounding in the very short term (early May) relative to the 0.55 anchor level. Furthermore, in post-inversion cycles, yield curves tend to structurally steepen as the Fed cuts rates or term premiums normalize, which provides upward support for the measure. Short-term momentum from the most recent three days is downward, but overall macro trends remain steepening. Abiding by the mandatory instruction that "uncertainty should INCREASE with forecast horizon — probabilities for distant dates should be closer to 0.5", I start with a higher assigned probability for the near-term May 4 date and progressively scale probabilities closer to 0.5 for the later resolution dates.
